title: Jump Game
description: You are given an array of non-negative integers nums, where each element represents your maximum jump length at that position. Your goal is to determine if you can reach the last index starting from the first index. You can assume that you can always reach the last index if you can jump to it directly.

examples.explanation: From index 0, you can jump to index 1 (3) or index 2 (1). Jumping to index 1, you can then reach index 4 directly.
From index 0, you can jump to index 1 (2) or index 2 (1) but cannot reach the last index (4) since index 1 and index 2 both lead to numbers that cannot jump far enough.
You are already at the last index since there is only one element in the array.

constraints: The length of nums is in the range [1, 10^4].
Each nums[i] is a non-negative integer not exceeding 10^5.
hints: Consider how far you can jump from each index.
Keep track of the furthest index you can reach as you iterate through the array.
If at any point your current index exceeds the furthest reachable index, you cannot reach the end.
Try to use a greedy approach to optimize your checks for reachable indexes.
